title: local-seo-agency: test coverage gaps + format.ts/rankings facts
content: local-seo-agency (Next.js 16 + React 19 + TS-strict, ESM) had ZERO tests. ADR-0001 (docs/adr/) recommends node --test via tsx as the `test` script (11 pts, 5 tasks). Key domain facts: (1) src/lib/format.ts formatSignedCurrency uses a typographic minus U+2212 ('−'), NOT ASCII hyphen — any test or string-match must use the right glyph. (2) format functions do not guard NaN → '$NaN'/'NaN%' leak to UI. (3) src/features/rankings has NO pure computation — data.ts is static mock data; the only ranking-delta (prev-now) is inline in KeywordTable.tsx and a latent defect renders rank DROPS as '—' identical to no-change, hiding regressions. Recommend extracting rankDelta() helper. Map-pack/share-of-voice are pure presentation, nothing to test.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:15 UTC
local-seo-agency rankings: confirmed rank-drop display defect + format.ts NaN gap
content: Code review of local-seo-agency confirmed a HIGH correctness defect in src/features/rankings/components/KeywordTable.tsx (lines 25,35-42): change = prev - now is rendered only as '↑ N' when >0 else '—', so rank DROPS (now>prev, change<0) render identically to no-change, hiding regressions. Currently LATENT — all data.ts mock rows have prev>=now. Fix = extract pure rankDelta(prev,now) and render '↓ N' in danger color. Separately, src/lib/format.ts has no Number.isFinite guard, so formatSignedCurrency(NaN)->'$NaN' and formatPercent(NaN)->'NaN%' leak to UI; and all 4 formatters have ZERO call sites (dead code). Verdict was REQUEST_CHANGES. Signatures: KeywordTable.tsx:42#rank-drop-hidden, format.ts:20#nan-unguarded.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:18 UTC
local-seo-agency first security scan: 3 Medium, 0 Critical/High
content: First security scan of local-seo-agency (Next 16/React 19, C:/Users/mkdol/xprice/local-seo-agency). Findings: (M1) src/app/page.tsx:66-67 uses dangerouslySetInnerHTML but on HARDCODED static literals only — currently no XSS, latent risk because product is meant to render AI-generated GBP posts + user review text; signature page.tsx:66#dangerous-innerhtml-static. (M2) postcss <8.5.10 transitive via next, CVE GHSA-qx2v-qp2m-jg93 CVSS 6.1 XSS — fix via npm update postcss + dedupe, NOT npm's spurious next@9.3.3 downgrade; signature package-lock#postcss-xss. (M3) next moderate advisory, same postcss chain. No secrets tracked in git; .env.example has empty key names only; .gitignore covers .env/.gcp/*.pem; zero process.env or API routes in repo. App is static marketing + mock-data dashboard — no backend yet, so server-side injection/authz/PII checks deferred to next scan once Gemini/Stripe/OAuth/DATABASE_URL land.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:22 UTC
local-seo-agency release decision: v0.2.0 (MINOR) for platform upgrade
content: First recorded release decision for local-seo-agency (C:/Users/mkdol/xprice/local-seo-agency, private Next.js app). Merge 2cd0814 'Upgrade Next.js dependencies' bumped Next 14->16, React 18->19, Tailwind 3->4, TS 5->6, ESLint 9->10, @google/generative-ai 0.21->0.24.1, plus config migration (postcss.config.mjs, globals.css @import tailwindcss, tsconfig jsx react-jsx). DECISION: MINOR, 0.1.0 -> 0.2.0. RATIONALE: pre-1.0 (0.x) convention — breaking-style changes increment MINOR, not MAJOR; substantial migration so not a PATCH; no new product features and no public API (private:true) so not a feature-MINOR but the breaking-platform-MINOR. This matched a pre-existing CHANGELOG [0.2.0] entry — consistency preserved. Created annotated tag v0.2.0 on merge commit. PRECEDENT: for this repo while pre-1.0, treat major dependency/toolchain upgrades requiring migration as MINOR bumps.

title: Session capture (Stop) — 2026-05-27 11:26 UTC
v0.2.1 release event names a different repo (immigration-paperwork) — no-op
content: On 2026-05-27 a release.version.bumped event fired for version 0.2.1 (claiming previous 0.1.0) but with repo='immigration-paperwork' — NOT the connected codebase, which is 'Local SEO Agency' (xprice/local-seo-agency). The connected repo's HEAD is still 2cd0814 (the v0.2.0 watermark), latest tag v0.2.0, zero new commits. Two reasons for no-op: repo mismatch AND HEAD unchanged. This escalates a recurring pattern: the upstream Release Manager's payloads are disconnected from this codebase (prior v0.2.0 re-fires carried nonexistent commits 73b4573/18fab0f/0c2f2a4). Either this Docs Steward should be repointed at immigration-paperwork or events are misrouted — flag for the user. Always reconcile payload repo+commit against the connected codebase before acting.
